summary: VectorBuilder transfection experiment report testing three vectors (two clinical-compatible, one control) in Calu-6 cancer cell line. Western blot analysis demonstrates successful p53 protein expression 72 hours post-transfection in all three experimental vectors and G418 selection group, with fluorescence imaging confirming transfection efficiency.

content_blocks.title: Title and Metadata
content_blocks.content: Document: Customized transfection test with 3 vectors in Calu-6 cell line. Reference IDs: S250924-1038amb/P250903-1038mva. Date: 2025/10/28. Provider: VectorBuilder.

content_blocks.title: Table of Contents
content_blocks.content: Report sections: Materials, Protocol, Images, WB results (Western Blot), Conclusion

content_blocks.title: Materials and Methods Overview
content_blocks.content: Cell line: Calu-6 (from ATCC, passage 4) - a human lung cancer cell line. Cell density: 6.5×10^5 cells transfected (4×10^5 cells/well plated one day prior). Format: 6-well plates. Vectors tested: (1) VB240514-1548ggn (Control Vector), (2) VB250610-1157tpv (Clinical compatible vector 1), (3) VB250610-1162rmu (Clinical compatible vector 2), (4) VB181204-1110ybw pRP[Exp]-mCherry-EF1A>EGFP (Control with fluorescent markers). Primary antibody: p53(DO-1): sc-126 at 1:1000 dilution. Secondary antibody: HRP-labeled Goat Anti-Mouse IgG(H+L), Beyotime A0216 at 1:5000 dilution. Additional control: G418 selection at 5000 μg/mL.

content_blocks.title: Western Blot Protocol
content_blocks.content: Harvest time: 72 hours post-transfection. Cell lysis: 100μl RIPA buffer per 24-well plate. Sample treatment: Ultrasonic disruption followed by 95°C for 10 minutes. Protein loaded: 30μg per lane. SDS-PAGE conditions: 80V for 30 minutes, then 125V for 1 hour. Transfer: 250mA for 60 minutes. Primary antibody incubation: Overnight at 4°C. Secondary antibody incubation: 1 hour at room temperature. Experimental groups: M (marker), Lane 1: Calu6 WT (untransfected), Lane 2: VB240514-1548ggn, Lane 3: VB250610-1157tpv, Lane 4: VB250610-1162rmu, Lane 5: VB181204-1110ybw, Lane 6: G418 5000μg/mL treatment.

content_blocks.title: Fluorescence Microscopy Images - 72 Hours Post-Transfection
content_blocks.content: Imaging parameters: Bright field 10ms, Green fluorescence 100ms, Red fluorescence 100ms. Results: Calu6 WT (no fluorescence), VB240514-1548ggn (no visible fluorescence), VB250610-1157tpv (no visible fluorescence), VB250610-1162rmu (no visible fluorescence), VB181204-1110ybw (strong green EGFP and red mCherry fluorescence signals indicating successful transfection), G418 treatment (no fluorescence). Images show confluent cell monolayers with only the dual-fluorescent control vector demonstrating expression.

content_blocks.title: Western Blot Results - p53 Protein Expression
content_blocks.content: Three exposure times shown: 0.5s (GAPDH loading control, 40kDa band visible in all lanes), 7s (p53 detection at ~55kDa), 10s (p53 detection with higher sensitivity). GAPDH: Strong, uniform 40kDa bands across all 6 samples confirming equal protein loading. p53 (55kDa): Lane 1 (WT): minimal/no p53 signal. Lane 2 (VB240514-1548ggn control vector): clear p53 band. Lane 3 (VB250610-1157tpv clinical vector 1): strong p53 band. Lane 4 (VB250610-1162rmu clinical vector 2): strong p53 band. Lane 5 (VB181204-1110ybw fluorescent control): faint/minimal p53 band. Lane 6 (G418 treatment): strong p53 band. Results demonstrate successful p53 expression from both clinical-compatible vectors at comparable or higher levels than control vector.

content_blocks.title: Conclusions
content_blocks.content: Key findings: (1) Fluorescence imaging confirmed successful plasmid transfection based on VB181204-1110ybw expressing clear EGFP and mCherry signals at 72 hours. (2) Western blot detected p53 expression in all three experimental vector transfection groups (VB240514-1548ggn, VB250610-1157tpv, VB250610-1162rmu), confirming functional gene delivery. (3) p53 expression also detected in G418 selection group, validating the experimental approach. Overall assessment: Experiment was successful - both clinical-compatible vectors (VB250610-1157tpv and VB250610-1162rmu) successfully delivered and expressed p53 protein in Calu-6 human lung cancer cells in vitro.

key_findings: In vitro proof-of-concept: Two clinical-compatible vectors successfully transfect Calu-6 human lung cancer cells and express p53 protein at 72 hours post-transfection
Cell line validation: Calu-6 (ATCC, passage 4) confirmed as suitable model for vector testing with reproducible transfection

Quantitative protein expression: Western blot shows p53 bands at expected 55kDa molecular weight in all three test vectors, with clinical vectors showing strong expression comparable to or exceeding control vector
Transfection efficiency demonstrated: Control vector with dual fluorescent markers (EGFP/mCherry) shows robust expression confirming cell permeability and transcriptional activity
Selection marker validation: G418 treatment control also shows p53 expression, supporting stable transfection capability

TRL indicator: This represents in vitro cell culture validation (TRL 2-3 boundary) - functional gene delivery to disease-relevant cancer cell line with protein expression confirmation, but limited to single cell line and single timepoint without functional outcome measures (apoptosis, proliferation, tumor suppression)
